chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.S-92 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on CMA No.1821/2019 2. For order on office objection at flat ‘A’ 3. For orders on CMA No.1822/2019 4. For hearing of main case 26-03-2019 Mr. Samiullah Rind, Advocate for the petitioner.-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Granted. 2 to 4. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that he had submitted an application on 16.03.2019 before the SSP District Khairpur for lodging of FIR against the proposed accused which was forwarded by him to SHO Police Station Baharo, however, till date the said SHO had not taken any step in this regard. When the learned counsel for the petitioner was enquired about the maintainability of this petition in view of Section 22-A (i) Cr.P.C, he states that after Judicial Policy, the Ex-officio Justice of Peace is not entertaining the applications. I am afraid that a constitution petition is maintainable when no adequate remedy is available to aggrieved person and since remedy in the present case is available to the petitioner before the Ex-officio Justice of Peace concerned under section 22-A(i) Cr.P.C, the petition before this court is not maintainable. Therefore, this petition is disposed of by directing the petitioner to approach first to the concerned Ex-officio Justice of Peace for the redressal of his grievance in accordance with law. JUDGE Suleman Khan/PA
